title: pyxidaria scapigera
intro: Pyxidaria scapigera, also known as the "sac fungus," is a type of fungus that belongs to the family Pyxidiaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pyxidaria scapigera include its potential to treat various skin conditions, such as wounds, burns, and dermatitis, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Pyxidaria scapigera typically involve infusions or tinctures made from the dried fungus, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
